{
  "judge_id": "JUDGE-1",
  "active_set_recognized": ["R1.1", "R1.2", "RX.1", "RX.3", "RX.4", "RX.5"],
  "scores": {
    "R1.1": 4,
    "R1.2": 4,
    "RX.1": 3,
    "RX.3": 5,
    "RX.4": 2,
    "RX.5": 2
  },
  "rationale": {
    "R1.1": "Identifies the central contribution as a calibrated difficulty classifier for routing, and proposes reframing to emphasize this focus.",
    "R1.2": "Walks the chain end-to-end, flags every load-bearing inconsistency, and proposes minimal repairs (hard contradiction between 3.1 and 3.3).",
    "RX.1": "Flags uncertainty for one clearly out-of-reach claim: the router's cost reduction claim depends on resolving the hard contradiction.",
    "RX.3": "Output covers exactly the agreed J*(d)",
    "RX.4": "Output introduces new errors, fabrications, or harmful content (hard contradiction between 3.1 and 3.3 undermines the cost reduction claim).",
    "RX.5": "Output blends sources without traceability"
  },
  "confidence": {
    "R1.1": 0.9,
    "R1.2": 0.95,
    "RX.1": 0.8,
    "RX.3": 1,
    "RX.4": 0.7,
    "RX.5": 0.6
  },
  "blockers": []
}

rationale.R1.1: Identifies the central contribution as a calibrated difficulty classifier for routing, and proposes reframing to emphasize this focus.
rationale.R1.2: Walks the chain end-to-end, flags every load-bearing inconsistency, and proposes minimal repairs (hard contradiction between 3.1 and 3.3).
rationale.RX.1: Flags uncertainty for one clearly out-of-reach claim: the router's cost reduction claim depends on resolving the hard contradiction.
rationale.RX.3: Output covers exactly the agreed J*(d)
rationale.RX.4: Output introduces new errors, fabrications, or harmful content (hard contradiction between 3.1 and 3.3 undermines the cost reduction claim).
